name: PinkPantheress
description:
'''Victoria Beverley Walker''' (born 19 April 2001), known professionally as '''PinkPantheress''', is a British singer-songwriter and record producer. Her music blends styles such as [[Contemporary R&B|R&B]], [[drum and bass]], [[UK garage]], [[house music|house]], and [[alternative pop]], often [[Sampling (music)|sampling]] music from the 1990s and 2000s and featuring affable [[stream-of-consciousness]] lyrics.

Born in [[Bath, Somerset]], and raised in [[Kent]], PinkPantheress began her musical career in 2021 while attending university in [[London]], where she produced songs using [[GarageBand]] and posted them on [[SoundCloud]] and [[TikTok]]. Several of them, including "[[Break It Off (PinkPantheress song)|Break It Off]]", became popular on TikTok, and she signed to [[Parlophone]] and [[Elektra Records]] and released her debut mixtape ''[[To Hell with It]]'' later that year. Her 2022 singles "[[Just for Me]]" and "[[Pain (PinkPantheress song)|Pain]]" peaked in the top 40 of the [[UK Singles Chart]], and "[[Boy's a Liar]]" reached number two.

PinkPantheress's debut studio album, ''[[Heaven Knows (PinkPantheress album)|Heaven Knows]]'' (2023), charted in ten countries. It spawned the UK top-20 song "[[Nice to Meet You (PinkPantheress song)|Nice to Meet You]]" and the remix single "[[Boy's a Liar Pt. 2]]" (with [[Ice Spice]]), which peaked at number three on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]]. She continued this success with her second mixtape, ''[[Fancy That]]'' (2025), which reached number three on the [[UK Albums Chart]]. It spawned the UK top-40 singles "[[Tonight (PinkPantheress song)|Tonight]]" and "[[Stateside (song)|Stateside]]", and "[[Illegal (PinkPantheress song)|Illegal]]" became her second entry in the US. The remix single "[[Stateside (song)#Zara Larsson remix|Stateside + Zara Larsson]]" became her second top-ten hit in the US and her first to top the [[Billboard Global 200|''Billboard'' Global 200]].

PinkPantheress won [[BBC]]'s [[Sound of...#Sound of 2022|Sound of 2022]] poll and was named [[Billboard Women in Music#Producer of the Year|Producer of the Year]] by [[Billboard Women in Music|''Billboard'' Women in Music]] in 2024. She won the [[Brit Awards|Brit Award]] for [[Brit Award for British Producer of the Year|British Producer of the Year]] in 2026, becoming the youngest person and first woman in history to be awarded the honour. She has also been nominated for three [[American Music Awards]] and two [[Grammy Awards]].
